meanings.definition: the state of demanding notice or attention
examples: the insistence of their hunger
the press of business matters

meanings.definition: the print media responsible for gathering and publishing news in the form of newspapers or magazines
a machine used for printing
a dense crowd of people
a tall piece of furniture that provides storage space for clothes; has a door and rails or hooks for hanging clothes
clamp to prevent wooden rackets from warping when not in use
any machine that exerts pressure to form or shape or cut materials or extract liquids or compress solids
a weightlift in which the barbell is lifted to shoulder height and then smoothly lifted overhead
the act of pressing; the exertion of pressure
examples: he gave the button a press
he used pressure to stop the bleeding
at the pressing of a button

meanings.definition: exert pressure or force to or upon
examples: He pressed down on the boards
press your thumb on this spot

meanings.definition: force or impel in an indicated direction
examples: I urged him to finish his studies

meanings.definition: to be oppressive or burdensome; ,
examples: weigh heavily on the mind
Something pressed on his mind

meanings.definition: place between two surfaces and apply weight or pressure
examples: pressed flowers

meanings.definition: squeeze or press together
examples: she compressed her lips
the spasm contracted the muscle

meanings.definition: crowd closely
examples: The crowds pressed along the street

meanings.definition: create by pressing
examples: Press little holes into the soft clay

meanings.definition: be urgent
examples: This is a pressing problem

meanings.definition: exert oneself continuously, vigorously, or obtrusively to gain an end or engage in a crusade for a certain cause or person; be an advocate for
examples: The liberal party pushed for reforms
She is crusading for women's rights
The Dean is pushing for his favorite candidate

meanings.definition: press from a plastic
examples: press a record

meanings.definition: make strenuous pushing movements during birth to expel the baby
examples: `Now push hard,' said the doctor to the woman

meanings.definition: press and smooth with a heated iron
examples: press your shirts
she stood there ironing

meanings.definition: lift weights
examples: This guy can press 300 pounds

meanings.definition: ask for or request earnestly
examples: The prophet bid all people to become good persons
